text.title: Help and Documentation Rules and Agent Gotchas
text.summary: Authoring quality rules, measuring help effectiveness, common mistakes, and agent limitations for help content generation.

rule.statement: Test search discoverability by typing the phrase a frustrated user would type, not the feature name.
rule.rationale: Users search by task context ("why is my payment failing") not by feature name ("Payment Processing Module"); discoverability testing must use user-language queries.

rule.statement: Add a "Last reviewed" date to every help article and schedule quarterly reviews in the project backlog.
rule.rationale: Help content decays as the product changes; undated articles have no signal about staleness, and users lose trust when steps no longer match the UI.

rule.statement: Keep FAQs to five questions maximum per page; create separate detailed articles for depth.
rule.rationale: Long FAQ pages become unscannable; five questions per page with links to detail articles maintains scannability while enabling depth.

rule.statement: Link related articles bidirectionally; users reading "password reset" also need "two-factor authentication".
rule.rationale: Single-article dead ends force users back to search; bidirectional links keep users in the help flow until their question is answered.

section.title: Measuring Help Effectiveness
section.p: Help searches — what users are looking for (unmet needs signal). Search success rate — do users find answers? (gaps in coverage). Support tickets — questions that help should answer but does not. Feature adoption — does contextual help increase usage of complex features? Deflection rate — tickets created after reading an article = help failure.

antipattern.description: Help content not maintained after feature changes.
antipattern.reason: Outdated screenshots and broken step sequences are worse than no documentation; they waste user time and erode trust.

antipattern.description: Documentation written for experts, not users.
antipattern.reason: Jargon without explanation excludes the users who need help most; documentation must assume the minimum viable context.

antipattern.description: No help exists at the point of confusion — only a footer link to a help center.
antipattern.reason: Users who encounter confusion mid-task need help in context, not after navigating away and losing their progress.

rule.statement: Every agent-generated step sequence must be verified against actual product flows before publishing.
rule.rationale: LLMs generate plausible-sounding but incorrect step sequences without product access; factual accuracy requires human verification against the live product.

rule.statement: Articles touching billing, security, or account deletion require legal/compliance review before publishing.
rule.rationale: Incorrect information in these areas creates legal and reputational risk; human review is mandatory regardless of how accurate the agent output appears.

rule.statement: Instruct the agent to keep help articles under 500 words and audit length in a follow-up pass.
rule.rationale: LLMs over-enumerate steps; long articles are not read to completion and fail to answer the user's specific question quickly.

text.reference: Python script to find features missing help coverage
